title: [lora_l2.c:225-301] SECURITY: EUI-64 derived from hardware ID only - no key binding
description:
Security finding: The EUI-64 is derived from hwinfo_get_device_id() via SHA-256, but has no cryptographic binding to the node's identity keypair.

Attack scenario:
1. Two nodes with different hardware IDs have different EUI-64s
2. But their Ed25519 keypairs are independent of the EUI-64
3. An attacker with the victim's private key can sign frames with victim's EUI-64
4. Or: an attacker could claim any EUI-64 if they have ANY valid keypair

The LICHEN spec section 6.2 mentions deriving IID from Ed25519 pubkey, but generate_eui64() uses hardware ID instead.

Impact: Identity spoofing is possible if private keys are compromised. The EUI-64 provides no independent authentication.

Current state: Not immediately exploitable because keys aren't provisioned.

Mitigation:
1. Consider deriving EUI-64 from Ed25519 pubkey as spec section 6.2 suggests
2. Or: bind the hardware ID to the keypair during provisioning
3. Document that EUI-64 is NOT a cryptographic identity - pubkey is
close_reason: Documented: added SECURITY comment explaining EUI-64 is not cryptographically bound to keypair, Ed25519 pubkey is the true identity, future spec 6.2 compliance
